metadata.seed: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

patient.query: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

automatic_thoughts: I'm scared I'm going to get fired
I keep overthinking everything

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of worry and anxiety at work right now. It's understandable to feel scared when you're caught in a cycle of overthinking, especially when it leads to concerns about your job security. Let’s explore this a bit further.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... I feel like I'm constantly second-guessing myself. One wrong move, and I could be in trouble. It’s like this weight just hangs over me every day. I don’t want to let anyone down, but it feels like I'm in this endless loop of "what ifs."

automatic_thoughts: I feel like I'm constantly second-guessing myself
One wrong move, and I could be in trouble
It feels like I'm in this endless loop of 'what ifs'

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly draining to feel like you're constantly questioning yourself and worrying about making mistakes. That sense of pressure and those persistent "what ifs" can certainly feel like a heavy weight. It takes a lot of energy to carry that kind of burden.

patient.query: It really is draining. Sometimes I just want to shut it all out, but then I feel guilty for wanting to escape. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to do my best and being terrified of messing up. I don’t know, it just feels like I can’t win either way.

automatic_thoughts: I just want to shut it all out
I feel guilty for wanting to escape
I'm stuck between wanting to do my best and being terrified of messing up
I can't win either way

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel pulled in so many directions, and it's understandable that you'd feel drained. Wanting to shut things out and then feeling guilty afterward suggests a real struggle. Let’s explore what’s contributing to this sense of being trapped.

patient.query: It’s really exhausting. I find myself just overwhelmed with all these what-ifs, and then I feel guilty for even wanting a break from it. It’s like there’s this constant pressure to be perfect, but I’m so scared of failing that I don’t know how to move forward. I just feel stuck.

automatic_thoughts: what-ifs
pressure to be perfect
scared of failing
feel guilty for wanting a break

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy load right now, and it makes complete sense that you'd feel exhausted. Wanting a break when you're feeling overwhelmed is a natural response, and it's understandable to feel guilty when you're caught between those needs and the pressure you're putting on yourself. Let’s explore this a bit more.

patient.query: Yeah, it feels like this never-ending cycle. I want to be strong and keep pushing through, but I also feel like I'm going to break if I don't take a step back. Sometimes it just feels so unfair that I have to work this hard just to feel okay. I don’t know how to balance it all without feeling like I’m failing.

automatic_thoughts: this never-ending cycle
I have to work this hard just to feel okay
I’m failing

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly draining to feel caught between wanting to be strong and needing to protect yourself. It makes sense that you'd feel frustrated and like things are unfair when you're putting in so much effort just to feel alright. Let’s explore what’s contributing to this sense of overwhelm.

patient.query: It’s just so tiring. I feel like I have to be perfect all the time, and then when I mess up, it just crushes me. I wish I could take a break from all this pressure, but it makes me feel guilty. I just feel stuck, like there’s no way out of this heaviness.

automatic_thoughts: I have to be perfect all the time
it crushes me
I wish I could take a break
I feel guilty
I feel stuck
there's no way out of this heaviness

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly draining to feel that weight and pressure constantly. It makes sense that you'd wish for a break, especially when you're feeling overwhelmed and guilty. Let's explore what's contributing to this heaviness and see if we can find some ways to ease it.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. It feels like I’m always walking on eggshells, and I never know when I’ll crack. I guess part of me worries that if I take a break, everything will fall apart. It’s hard to see a way out when I feel like I’m holding everything together all by myself.

automatic_thoughts: everything will fall apart
I’m holding everything together all by myself

entailment: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly exhausting to feel like you're constantly navigating a delicate situation and carrying so much responsibility. It makes sense that you'd feel anxious when you're worried about things falling apart if you were to step back, even for a moment. Let’s explore what makes you feel like you’re the only one holding everything together.

patient.query: It’s just... I guess I’ve always felt like I had to be the strong one in my family. If I don’t keep things in line, then everything goes sideways. It’s like I’m on this tightrope, and if I slip, I don’t know what will happen. I feel like if I let go, no one else will step in, and that terrifies me.

triggers: feeling like I had to be the strong one
automatic_thoughts: if I slip, I don’t know what will happen
if I let go, no one else will step in

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)
Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly exhausting to carry that responsibility and feel like you're constantly balancing everything for your family. It makes sense that you'd feel terrified when you imagine things falling apart or letting go. It takes a lot of strength to acknowledge that feeling.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is exhausting. I just feel this weight all the time, and it’s hard to see anyone else stepping up. I know I should be able to lean on them, but it feels like they wouldn’t know how to handle it if I did. Like, if I showed any vulnerability, it would just add to the chaos. I wish I could find a way to stop carrying

automatic_thoughts: I should be able to lean on them
They wouldn’t know how to handle it if I did
If I showed any vulnerability, it would just add to the chaos

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly draining to feel that constant weight and responsibility. It takes a lot of strength to carry that kind of load, and it's understandable that you're feeling exhausted. It makes sense that you're hesitant to reach out, given those concerns.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel like I’m carrying the world sometimes. I just wish I could feel lighter, you know? But then I think about what would happen if I did lean on someone, and it just makes me spiral. I guess I just feel like it’s all on me, and I don’t know how to change that.

automatic_thoughts: I’m carrying the world
it’s all on me
I don’t know how to change that

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly heavy to feel that weight, and it's understandable that you'd want to feel lighter. It takes courage to acknowledge that feeling overwhelmed, and it’s natural to hesitate about reaching out for support when you’re already carrying so much. Let’s explore what makes you spiral when you consider leaning on someone.
